target: 7. Scouring the Island
We finally made it, Mt. Takanosu! The location on that memo, it's definitely here, no mistake!
You were saying the exact same thing when we went to the other places, too.
When we went to Alain's field, when we went to Torashima, and even when we went to <color=#GF_TIPS_044>Shinja Marsh</color>.
We've walked all over the island, and I'm exhausted... Right, Kaori-chan?
Ahaha... We really did walk all over the place.
I'm telling you, this time it's the real deal! Look, take a look at this memo!
I just remembered earlier. This drawing of a cat was of the black cat that used to hang around Mt. Takanosu.
See, remember when we came here for an outdoor class a long time ago? Don't you remember?

Oh…!
I remember!
It was the one sunbathing by the rocks, right? I had just finished reading a book with a black cat in it, so I remember it well.
Yeah, exactly! And when I got closer to try and pet that cat, I ended up slipping on the slope.
...I remember now. I was the one who went to help you then. And then we both got scolded.
You're always causing trouble for Shin-chan, aren't you, Sis?
That was because Shinpei yelled "Ushiooo!" so loudly that the teacher found out.
You...
More importantly, this stone here on the memo! This isn't just any pebble, either! It's the stone Shinpei threw!
Eh, I did?
Yeah, over there where the trees are lined up. There was a squirrel there.
Shinpei noticed that a snake was approaching it. He threw a stone and chased the snake away.
Hehe... that's so like Ajiro-kun.
Hmmm... I don't really remember that story...
So, the drawing of the tree on this memo refers to those standing trees over there?
Now that you mention it... I suppose it doesn't *not* look like that?
Great deduction, right? This memo was a map of Mt. Takanosu!
You're the one who wrote the memo in the first place, Sis. Why do you have to deduce it?
Besides, if you had remembered earlier, we wouldn't have needed to go to Torashima and all those other places...
It's because of struggles like that that you can feel the joy of getting the right answer.
You're saying something that sounds plausible, but weren't you just planning to go somewhere else if this place was a bust too?
Well, I won't deny that.
You really don't think things through, Sis.
Call it being adaptable!
*Sigh*... Try putting yourself in the shoes of the people getting dragged into it.
…Hehe!
Haha, ahahahahaha!
K-Koyuba-san?
Hehe, I'm sorry! It was just so funny, I couldn't help it! You three look like you're having so much fun.
We're always like this. Right, Shinpei?
Yeah, pretty much.
(Anyway... I'm glad Koyuba-san seems to be having fun.)
(She seemed a bit stiff at first, but now her expression seems much more relaxed.)
(Thinking about it that way... going back and forth all over the island might not have been a waste after all.)
Anyway, there's no doubt that the treasure is right in the middle of these three points!
Now all we have to do is pinpoint the location of the time capsule and dig it up! Shinpei, get out the shovel you brought!
Yeah, yeah.
Huh? Did something happen?
N-No, it's nothing... Let's go look for the time capsule too.
Yeah.
Look, we're almost there. The top of the hill is just ahead.
Everyone, watch your step. This area is a slope, and it's slippery!
Hyaa!
Hey, you're the one who needs to be careful.
That was a bad example! Anyway, I'm going on ahead!
Ah~, I'm starting to feel like we're really on an expedition or something.
We used to play like this a lot in the past too, huh.
We sure did. Sou would join us too, and we'd play make-believe explorers or tag around the gun battery ruins and places like that.
Hmm... so you guys used to play like that, Ajiro-kun.
Mio, come here for a second!
What is it, Sis!?
Ushio was always like this, huh. Saying we were going to make a secret base, and dragging us all over the place.
Hehe, I feel like I can picture it.
If I... had been as proactive back then as I am now, I could have been with Ajiro-kun and the others--
Look out!!

Phew, that was close.
Are you okay?
Y-Yeah... thank you.
It's slippery around here, so we have to be careful.
Even though Kofune-san just warned us about it... I'm sorry.
You don't need to apologize. It's muddy around here, anyway.
More importantly, are you hurt? If Koyuba-san were to get hurt, it would be a huge deal.
I-I'm fine. Thanks to you catching me, Ajiro-kun.
I see, that's good then.
U-Um, well...
Huh? What's wrong?
Um, thank you.
It's nothing to thank me for.
I was happy... and besides...
It was... like that back then, too.
Huh…?
No, it's nothing! More importantly...
Huh?
I'm okay now, so if you could let go...
A-Ah! Sorry! C-Can you stand on your own?
Y-Yeah, somehow... kya!
Hahaha, looks like you're slipping and it's not going to work.
I'm sorry.
Then, I'll lend you a hand.
Ah, thank you...
Hold onto my arm until we get somewhere easier to walk.
Th-Thank you...
Shinpei, what are you doing! Hurry up and come over here!
Ushio is calling. Let's go.
Y-Yeah.
*Sigh*~, we couldn't find it in the end, huh. The time capsule.
I was sure it was around there...
Just give up already... If we dig any more holes, I won't be able to move tomorrow from full-body muscle soreness.
Sorry, Kaori-chan. We couldn't find the time capsule.
Plus... even though it's before your live performance, we ended up making you unnecessarily tired.
No, I don't mind at all! It was so much fun.
But, at this rate, your memories...
Kofune-san, you said it yourself, didn't you? If you don't have any memories, we can just make them together.
It really turned out just like that. I was able to properly make memories with everyone today.
Exploring all over the island together, going on an adventure like a treasure hunt...
I had so much fun!!
Kaori-chan...
You know... I said earlier that when I lived on this island, I spent a lot of time alone, right.
Ah... yeah.
When I lived on Hitogashima, I often felt lonely.
But, today was different.
I was really happy that Kofune-san, Ajiro-kun, and Mio-chan kept me company all day today.
I was able to tour the island with a happy feeling, and learn about various charms of the island that I hadn't noticed in the past...
With these feelings, I think I'll be able to properly convey the charm of Hitogashima at the talk show.
So... thank you!
Thanks to everyone, today has become a very fun and memorable day!
Koyuba-san...
(It's not just my imagination.)
(Her smile looks so much brighter than when we first met.)
I see... I'm glad to hear that!
Well, it was all according to my calculations! It was worth going out of our way to tour the whole island.
Don't make it sound so good. If things had gone wrong, it might have just ended with us dragging her around.
Seriously. What were you planning to do if the live performance ended up getting canceled?
Ahaha, we'd cross that bridge when we come to it! Right, Kaori-chan?
Yeah! Exactly!
